item.title: Ever heard traditional Gaelic Irish music sung by an a cappella group like this? This is Ceann Dubh Dilis (My Sweet, Dark-Haired Love), arranged by Michael McGlynn and performed by Spokanki.

item.title: Turns out Milana Vayntrub isn't just hot, she's just plain awesome
item.description: Basically, the site is OnlyFans, but the money goes to help people. In the case of Milana, she's helping families who are suffering because of the LA forest fires. As she puts it, "using the male gaze to fight the blaze." I believe she's raised a couple hundred grand so far, and is aiming for more. https://brobible.com/culture/article/att-girl-milana-vayntrub-only-philanthropy-photos/ And if you're curious for more here's her Insta, give a click (for charity!): https://www.instagram.com/reel/DL5G2Hmu83f/?utm_source=ig_web_button_share_sheet
item.dc:creator: Huor

item.title: Rare pieces at the bone museum

item.title: I stayed up just to bring you Frank
item.description:
This wasn't a planned shot, just a quick click, but man, Frank is a natural.
While waiting for imgur to come back up, Frank fell asleep, then slid off my lap until he was delicately balanced on his nose like a really weird interpretation of Swan Lake
From earlier, his paw...the hint of beans
Here's the full thing. Tiny octopus is no tease.
I love when he falls asleep on my arm. It's like three pounds of pure trust.
Bonus Puddles tax.
Bonus Chessie tax. Good night Imgur. Hope your week was better than the server hamster's was. Remember to go outside and look at the full moon this weekend. It's amazing how beautiful something that we see every day can be if we just pay it a little more attention.
item.dc:creator: FrankTheKitten

item.dc:creator: dotnote

item.title: Let's send food to Imgurians in need
item.description:
MrPuckett here super excited for TheFreePizzaDude post!! If you'd like to help a struggling Imgurian get groceries or a meal today there are 2 easy ways -
You can find a request for food in the comments, reply to them and then send a DM to get an order set up Or you can contribute to the Imgurian Meal Fund and we'll send the food for you!
This has been such a huge help for us to feed more people and we love you so much https://www.paypal.me/TheFreePizzaDude https://ko-fi.com/thefreepizzadude
And here's how you'd send it yourself! Any questions as an Angel? Just shoot us a message! We'll be on this username until 7pm central
I took home Nanna 4 years ago this week, in her honor - here's how it works for requests
It's much easier for us to help requests in the US but we'll do our best to pick up requests from any country. Putting your country in your comment helps us match you to an Angel in your area
Any questions as a requestor? Just leave a comment! We'll have helpers on the post for 6 hours
15,629 meals since we started 1,129 grocery orders sent 6 more hours to help as many people as we can Let's get it going! ... Today's Meal Tally 135 1st, 2nd, 3rd and 4th by @OP 205 5th, 6th and 7th by Pizza Angel @TheCraicen 240 8th, 9th, 10th and 11th by @OP 340 12th and 13th by Pizza Angel @Evocan69 350 14th, 15th and 16th by Pizza Angel @AyatollahBahloni 355 17th, 18th, 19th and 20th by @OP 21st by Pizza Angel @KInADress 22nd, Michael Jordan, Kobe Bryant and 25th by @OP 26th and 27th by Pizza Angel @hwatl4bloopy ... Today's Grocery Tally 130 1st by Grocery Angel @TheCraicen 637 2nd by Grocery Angel @WeberQs ... Alright everyone that's it for @OP Today we sent out 27 meals Filled 2 huge grocery orders That's 15,656 meals since we started 1,131 grocery orders sent Well done everyone, see you next week!
item.dc:creator: TheFreePizzaDude

item.title: Cold Cased
item.description: The 2012 case involves Debbie Stevens, a Long Island woman who donated a kidney to help her boss, Jacqueline Brucia, move up the transplant list through a paired kidney exchange. Stevens worked as an administrative assistant at Atlantic Automotive Group (AAG), where Brucia was a controller. The events unfolded as follows: In 2010, Stevens offered to donate her kidney after learning of Brucia's need during a visit to the dealership. Despite not being a perfect match, Stevens donated her kidney to a stranger in Missouri in August 2011, enabling Brucia to receive a kidney from another donor. Stevens experienced complications post-surgery, including pain and digestive issues, and returned to work after four weeks, feeling pressured despite her condition. Stevens alleged that upon her return, Brucia treated her harshly, demoting her, transferring her to a distant dealership, and denying accommodations for her recovery. After consulting a psychiatrist and having her lawyers send a complaint to AAG in March 2012, she was fired within a week, with the company citing "performance issues." Stevens filed a complaint with the New York State Division of Human Rights, which found "probable cause" of discrimination in October 2012, paving the way for a potential $15 million lawsuit. AAG and Brucia denied the allegations, calling them "groundless" and asserting fair treatment. The case escalated to a federal lawsuit in 2013, alleging violations of the Americans with Disabilities Act, but was settled confidentially on September 30, 2014. The exact reasons for her firing—whether due to recovery time or other factors—were never adjudicated, leaving the narrative open to interpretation. Critically, the settlement suggests both sides may have sought to avoid a public trial, but it does not confirm the discrimination claims. The story's sensational aspects (e.g., firing after a heroic act) have fueled ongoing online outrage, though details remain disputed. No further legal or public updates exist as of July 11, 2025.
item.dc:creator: TheTaoofRob

item.title: Can i punch you... not in my PUB..

item.title: Ben Is Smart Enough Not to Ask Questions

item.title: New foster kitten!!
item.description: The humane society just texted that they found a box on their doorstep this morning with a single female kitten inside. “Spicy” was the word they used to describe her. The foster coordinator said that she is about ten weeks old and could use some socialization. She’s black with a tiny spot of white on her chest. Of course I said I would take her! Luckily I still have Waffles, who is going to work her magic and teach this little baby how to cat. I’m picking her up in two hours!! No name…suggestions welcome!
item.dc:creator: KittensForDays

item.title: A Book I Hope to Get My Hands On
item.description: 'In pursuit of counterrevolution and in the name of freedom, U.S. forces or U.S.-supported surrogate forces slaughtered 2,000,000 North Koreans in a three-year war; 3,000,000 Vietnamese; over 500,000 in aerial wars over Laos and Cambodia; over 1,500,000 in Angola; over 1,000,000 in Mozambique; over 500,000 in Afghanistan [as of the book's publication]; 500,000 to 1,000,000 in Indonesia; 200,000 in East Timor; 100,000 in Nicaragua (combining the Somoza and Reagan eras); over 100,000 in Guatemala (plus an additional 40,000 disappeared); over 700,000 in Iraq [again: as of the book's publication. The Iraq War of the 21st century had a death toll of over a million Iraqis.]; over 60,000 in El Salvador; 30,000 in the "dirty war" of Argentina (though the government admits to only 9,000); 35,000 in Taiwan, when Kuomintang military arrived from China; 20,000 in Chile; and many thousands in Haiti, Panama, Grenada, Brazil, South Africa, Western Sahara, Zaire, Turkey, and dozens of other countries, in what ammounts to a free-market world holocaust. Official sources either deny these U.S.-sponsored mass murders or justify them as necessary measures that had to be taken against an implacable communist foe.' - Michael Parenti, Blackshirts & Reds (San Francisco: City Light Books, 1997), p. 25
item.dc:creator: unwaver
